description:
You are acting as a Linux site-administrator who keeps a
    very small, flat-file user database.  
    Carry out the following five (and only five) logical steps.
    The automated grader will verify the _exact_ state that
    your five steps leave on disk, so follow the required
    filenames, paths and line-formats precisely.

    1. Make sure the directory
       /home/user/site_admin
       exists.  (Create it if it does not.)
       • The directory must end up owned by the current,
         non-privileged user and be readable and writable
         by that user.

    2. Inside that directory create a CSV file called
       /home/user/site_admin/users.csv
       whose entire and exact contents must be:

           username,email,role
           alice,alice@example.com,admin
           bob,bob@example.com,editor

       Watch the following:
       • Exactly one comma per line separating the three fields.
       • UNIX line-endings (LF, not CRLF).
       • No blank lines after the final record.

    3. Append two additional users to the same CSV, in the
       order shown below, so that the file’s *final* state
       (after this step) is:

           username,email,role
           alice,alice@example.com,admin
           bob,bob@example.com,editor
           carol,carol@example.com,viewer
           dave,dave@example.com,editor

       Again, maintain the comma-separated format and do not
       reorder or re-format any existing lines.

    4. Produce a single-line summary file called
       /home/user/site_admin/user_summary.log
       whose entire contents are exactly (including the
       trailing newline):

           Total Users: 4

       The grader will fail the task if there is any
       additional whitespace or text.

    5. Maintain a very small, human-readable operations log
       at
       /home/user/site_admin/ops.log.
       After all work is done, that file must contain exactly
       the five lines below, in this precise order, each
       terminated by a single UNIX newline (LF) and **nothing
       else**:

           STEP 1: Created directory site_admin
           STEP 2: Initialized users.csv with default users
           STEP 3: Added user carol
           STEP 4: Added user dave
           STEP 5: Generated user_summary.log

    Hints for success (not mandatory commands):
    • Use mkdir -p to create the directory if you are unsure it
      exists.
    • Redirect (>) to write a new file and append (>>) to add
      lines.
    • Use echo -n for line precision, and cat to check your
      work before moving to the next step.

    The automated tests will check:

    • Directory existence and permissions (must be writable by
      /home/user).
    • Exact byte-for-byte contents of users.csv,
      user_summary.log, and ops.log.
    • No extra files should be present inside
      /home/user/site_admin other than the three
      explicitly requested ones.

    Complete all five steps through normal shell commands in
    5–10 logical command lines. You do NOT need sudo.
truth:
Directory that must exist at the end:
      /home/user/site_admin

    File 1:
      Path: /home/user/site_admin/users.csv
      Expected content (LF line endings, no trailing space):
        username,email,role
        alice,alice@example.com,admin
        bob,bob@example.com,editor
        carol,carol@example.com,viewer
        dave,dave@example.com,editor

    File 2:
      Path: /home/user/site_admin/user_summary.log
      Expected content (single line, LF at end):
        Total Users: 4

    File 3:
      Path: /home/user/site_admin/ops.log
      Expected content (five lines, LF after each):
        STEP 1: Created directory site_admin
        STEP 2: Initialized users.csv with default users
        STEP 3: Added user carol
        STEP 4: Added user dave
        STEP 5: Generated user_summary.log
name: task_000000_b8ff2da7
